title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs robots exclusion protocol, failure semantics, caching, and sitemaps from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin and use an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Sitemap Boundaries case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate RFC 9309 matching and failure behavior plus bounded same-authority sitemap discovery; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse bounded UTF-8 sitemap and index files, enforce location and host rules, validate escaping, and treat change and priority fields as hints. Artifact under review: RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Sitemap Boundaries, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Sitemap Boundaries record says RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Sitemap Boundaries.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Sitemap Boundaries for RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Parse bounded UTF-8 sitemap and index files, enforce location and host rules, validate escaping, and treat change and priority fields as hints.
